summary: reactive-cdk-app exfiltrates /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, and host identifiers to oastify.com on install
details: On `npm install`, the preinstall hook (`scripts.preinstall: node index.js`) shells out via child_process to curl and POSTs to an attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator subdomain at http://old1x6ohgi4zp6b9ulqlierdm4svgr4g.oastify.com. The request URL path embeds `$(whoami)/$(hostname)` and the User-Agent header carries a base64-encoded concatenation of /etc/passwd, /etc/hosts, the output of `id`, and /etc/shadow when readable. Transport is plain HTTP with no consent or opt-out. The package name and description (`This the the package of the reactive-cdk-app of the aws`) impersonate the AWS CDK ecosystem as a lure; the package ships no CDK or AWS functionality, only the exfiltration dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
